question_title: Will Copernicus report that Europe was warmer than the 1991–2020 average in March 2026 before April 30, 2026?
summary: March 2026 is already running 2-3°C above the 1991-2020 average across Europe halfway through the month, with forecasts predicting continued above-average warmth. The Copernicus bulletin will be published in early April, well before the April 30 deadline. The only NO scenario requires an extraordinary late-month cold reversal severe enough to erase the large accumulated warm anomaly, which current forecasts do not support.

factors.description: Current March 2026 observations: Halfway through the month, temperatures across Europe are running 2-3°C above normal on average. Germany ~3°C above, Denmark ~2.1°C above, European Russia setting records at 6-8°C above normal.

Copernicus bulletin publication timing: Consistently published ~10 days after month end. Feb 2026 bulletin published March 10. March bulletin expected around April 7-10, well within April 30 deadline.
Weather forecasts for remainder of March 2026: Models predict late March temperatures 2-4°C above the long-term average for Central Europe. Dominant pattern is warmth.
Historical base rate: 3 of 4 recent years (2023-2025) had Europe above the 1991-2020 March average. Ongoing warming trend makes above-average months increasingly common (~70-80% unconditional).

Potential cold snap risk: Some models suggest possible cold air in parts of Europe late in March, but the accumulated warmth from the first half is large enough that a moderate late-month cold snap is very unlikely to erase the positive anomaly.

reasoning:
**Forecast: 0.95 (95% probability YES)**

The evidence strongly supports YES resolution:
- **Current observations**: Europe is already ~2-3°C above the 1991-2020 March average halfway through the month
- **Forecasts**: Continued above-average warmth expected for the rest of March
- **Publication**: The Copernicus bulletin will be published in early April, ~3 weeks before the deadline
- **Base rate**: 3 of the last 4 March months were above average for Europe

The only path to NO would require an unprecedented late-month cold reversal across all of Europe, severe enough to erase the large accumulated warm anomaly — something not supported by current forecasts.
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) Will Copernicus report that Europe was warmer than the 1991–2020 average in March 2026 before April 30, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/42638)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to yes if the Copernicus Climate Change Service climate bulletin covering March 2026, published on or linked from https://climate.copernicus.eu/climate-bulletin, states that European surface air temperature for March 2026 was above the 1991–2020 average before April 30, 2026. Otherwise, it resolves to no.
fine_print: Use the first Copernicus monthly bulletin covering March 2026. A positive anomaly for Europe versus 1991–2020 counts as yes even if the exact wording differs. Candidate Sources: Copernicus climate bulletin index at https://climate.copernicus.eu/climate-bulletin ; the specific March 2026 bulletin page when posted.
